bridge_mechanisms.E-H1: Quantitative variance-component decomposition of TUR sub-system noise (counting vs. spatial vs. C+D)
bridge_mechanisms.E-H4: Spatially heterogeneous DnaA-ATP counting current via CL/PG membrane-affinity gradient yielding pole-biased origin firing order
bridge_mechanisms.E-H2: RIDA kinetic timing window: rate-accuracy tradeoff with bidirectional U-shaped CV response to Hda titration
bridge_mechanisms.E-H7: Min Pareto-frontier TUR: non-monotonic precision with pattern instability bifurcation above critical MinD density

evolution_quality_check.E-H1_rationale: H1 predicted CV >= 9.5% floor. E-H1 adds: two-regime model (counting-dominated fast growth / C+D-dominated slow growth), three independent experimental decomposition protocols, and specific quantitative inequality (CV^2_counting > 50% of CV^2_total at fast growth). Mechanism specificity increased substantially.
evolution_quality_check.E-H4_rationale: H4 was blocked by 2023 PRX Life prior art (shared pool model). E-H4 replaces shared-pool bridge with spatial-gradient bridge, makes OPPOSITE prediction to 2023 paper (pole-first firing order vs random synchrony), and puts the Critic's DnaA membrane-interaction objection at the center of the mechanism.
evolution_quality_check.E-H2_rationale: H2 Landauer framing non-discriminating at 30x above bound. E-H2 introduces U-shaped CV-vs-Hda prediction (both knockdown AND overexpression increase CV) that is NOT predicted by Landauer, simpler reset, or any existing model. Directly addresses Critic demand for discriminating prediction.
evolution_quality_check.E-H7_rationale: H7 ignored Fei & Bhatt 2015 non-monotonicity, producing naive 15-25x floor estimate. E-H7 makes the non-monotonicity the central prediction via Pareto-frontier TUR; introduces bifurcation-coincidence test; revises efficiency estimate to ~3-5x above pattern-instability-limited floor.

hypotheses.parent_title: DnaA-ATP Counting at oriC Is Near-Optimal for the Bacterial Adder
hypotheses.title: Variance-Component Decomposition of the E. coli Adder Reveals DnaA Counting as the Dominant TUR-Bounded Noise Source at Fast Growth, with C+D Period Fluctuations Dominating at Slow Growth
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Quantitative variance-component decomposition of TUR sub-system noise (counting vs. spatial vs. C+D period) with growth-rate-dependent regime transition
hypotheses.weakness_addressed: Noise decomposition — what fraction of total CV comes from DnaA counting vs. Min vs. growth? Without decomposition, near-TUR-optimality is unobservable.
key_mechanistic_advances: Decomposes CV^2_added = CV^2_counting + CV^2_spatial + CV^2_period + CV^2_extrinsic
Predicts CV^2_counting > 50% of total at fast growth (> 1.5 dbl/hr)
Predicts phase transition from counting-dominated to C+D-dominated noise at 0.8-1.0 dbl/hr

Three independent experimental protocols: Min spatial isolation (delta-minCDE + SlmA anchor), C+D period measurement (DAPI flow cytometry), counting noise direct measurement (DnaA single-molecule tracking)
hypotheses.discriminating_prediction: CV^2_counting / CV^2_added > 0.5 at fast growth (>1.5 dbl/hr); < 0.3 at slow growth (< 0.5 dbl/hr). Phase transition at 0.8-1.0 dbl/hr. Falsified if Min spatial error accounts for > 30% of total variance (ΔminCDE + SlmA reduces CV_added from 10% to < 7%).
hypotheses.experimental_timeline: Phase transition measurement: 3-6 months (mother-machine + DAPI flow cytometry). Single-molecule DnaA tracking: 6-12 months.
hypotheses.groundedness: MEDIUM — all three variance components have published measurement methods; the specific fractional contributions are predicted, not yet measured
hypotheses.why_stronger: H1 gave a lower bound (CV >= 9.5%). E-H1 gives a fractional constraint (counting noise > 50% of total at fast growth) and a two-regime structure with a phase transition — substantially more informative and testable.

hypotheses.parent_title: Multi-Fork Replication Creates Parallel Counting Currents with Correlated Noise
hypotheses.title: DnaA-ATP Membrane-Affinity Gradient Creates Systematically Ordered Multi-Origin Firing at Fast Growth: Pole-Proximal Origins Fire Before Midcell-Proximal Origins Due to CL/PG-Enriched DnaA Recharging Microdomains
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Spatially heterogeneous DnaA-ATP counting current via CL/PG membrane-affinity gradient yielding pole-biased origin firing order — OPPOSITE prediction to 2023 PRX Life shared-pool model
hypotheses.weakness_addressed: 2023 PRX Life paper is direct prior art on shared-pool correlation. DnaA membrane affinity ignored in cytoplasmic pool model.
key_mechanistic_advances: CL/PG enrichment at bacterial poles creates spatial gradient in DnaA-ADP recharging rate (Sekimizu & Kornberg 1988, Mileykovskaya & Dowhan 2009)
Pole-proximal origins accumulate DnaA-ATP faster, fire FIRST systematically (3-8 min lead time)
Heterogeneous multi-current TUR: CV^2_pole < CV^2_midcell; population CV higher than homogeneous pool predicts
DnaA overexpression homogenizes spatial gradient — converges to 2023 PRX Life model in high-DnaA limit
hypotheses.discriminating_prediction: In cells with n=2 origins: >60% frequency of pole-proximal origin firing first (SeqA-GFP timing, dual-color ParS/HALO + ParS/SNAP at ori1 and 1800kb locus). CL deletion (ΔclsABC) should: (1) randomize firing order (2) increase CV_added 15-25%. 2023 PRX Life model predicts CL deletion has no effect.
hypotheses.experimental_timeline: Firing order measurement: 3-6 months (existing dual-color strain construction). CL deletion test: 2-4 months (existing ΔclsABC strains available).
hypotheses.groundedness: MEDIUM — DnaA membrane affinity [GROUNDED: Sekimizu & Kornberg 1988]; CL/PG pole enrichment [GROUNDED: Mileykovskaya & Dowhan 2009, Renner & Weibel 2011]; spatial gradient magnitude [PARAMETRIC: derived, not measured]
hypotheses.why_stronger: Completely novel bridge mechanism — spatial gradient from membrane lipid asymmetry — absent from 2023 PRX Life prior art. Makes opposite prediction (systematic pole-first ordering vs. random synchrony). Converts the Critic's membrane interaction objection into the central mechanistic element.

hypotheses.parent_title: RIDA-Mediated ATP Hydrolysis as Landauer Erasure Counter-Reset
hypotheses.title: RIDA Rate-Optimization Creates a Kinetic Timing Window for DnaA Counting Precision: A U-Shaped CV-vs-Hda Titration Curve Discriminates Rate-Accuracy Tradeoff from Landauer Erasure
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: RIDA kinetic timing window: bidirectional rate-accuracy tradeoff predicting U-shaped CV_added response to Hda titration (both knockdown AND overexpression increase CV)
hypotheses.weakness_addressed: Landauer framing uninformative at 30x above erasure bound. Predictions non-discriminating between Landauer and simpler counter-reset explanations.
key_mechanistic_advances: Kinetic window bounded by two constraints: (1) incomplete reset at low k_RIDA (f = exp(-tau_gen / tau_half_RIDA) fraction survives, CV^2 x (1-f)^-2); (2) premature erasure at high k_RIDA (r events prematurely hydrolyzed, CV^2 x [N_eff/(N_eff-r)]^2 with N_eff=11)
Beta-clamp residence at oriC provides automatic 3-5 min timing window for RIDA termination
U-shaped CV_added response to Hda titration: minimum at 1x WT, increase at both 0.1x and 10x
Growth-rate-dependent U-shape shift: at slow growth, premature erasure arm (overexpression) flattens
hypotheses.discriminating_prediction: Tunable-Hda (Para-inducible, Δhda background, Maduike et al. 2014 system) titrated 0.1x to 10x WT. Expected: CV_added minimum at 1x WT (~10%); CV ~14-16% at 0.1x Hda (incomplete reset); CV ~13-15% at 10x Hda (premature erasure). Hda overexpression INCREASING CV is uniquely predicted by kinetic window model — NOT by Landauer (predicts plateau at high Hda), NOT by simple reset model (predicts monotonic decrease).
hypotheses.experimental_timeline: 2-4 months. Existing Δhda strain + inducible Para-hda construct from Maduike et al. 2014 — immediate availability.
hypotheses.groundedness: MEDIUM — RIDA mechanism and beta-clamp coupling [GROUNDED: Kato & Katayama 2001; Camara et al. 2005]; tunable Hda system [GROUNDED: Maduike et al. 2014]; U-shape magnitude [PARAMETRIC: derived from kinetic window model]
hypotheses.why_stronger: Replaces non-discriminating Landauer framing with kinetic model generating genuinely novel bidirectional prediction. Hda overexpression arm (increases CV) is the key discriminating test — uniquely predicted. Directly answers the Critic's explicit demand.

hypotheses.parent_title: MinCDE Oscillation Dissipation Sets TUR Floor for Division Site Positioning
hypotheses.operation: Crossover (H7 periodic TUR + Fei & Bhatt 2015 non-monotonic dissipation finding)
hypotheses.title: MinCDE Dissipation-Precision Tradeoff Follows a Pareto Frontier with Precision Optimum at Wild-Type MinD Density: Resolution of Fei & Bhatt Non-Monotonicity via TUR Extended to Oscillatory Pattern Instability
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Min Pareto-frontier TUR: standard TUR applies below pattern-instability bifurcation (C_MinD < C*); above C*, pattern instability worsens precision despite increased dissipation. Wild-type at Pareto optimum.
hypotheses.weakness_addressed: H7 ignored Fei & Bhatt 2015 finding that excess MinD HURTS precision (contradicts naive TUR). Also: 15-25x efficiency estimate vague and unquantified. Critic flagged weakly falsifiable.
key_mechanistic_advances: Three MinD oscillation modes: traveling wave (WT, precise), standing wave (2x overexpression, imprecise), spatiotemporal chaos (5x, disordered)
Pareto-frontier TUR: CV^2 x Sigma = 2 holds below C* (Turing bifurcation); above C*, CV^2 increases despite increasing Sigma due to mode switching
Wild-type MinD sits at Pareto optimum — at pattern-instability bifurcation threshold
Revised efficiency estimate: ~3-5x above pattern-instability-limited Pareto floor (not 15-25x as H7 claimed)
Bifurcation coincidence prediction: C* (measured by oscillation pattern Fourier spectrum) should equal MinD concentration at U-curve minimum
hypotheses.discriminating_prediction: CRISPRi titration of MinD across 0.1x to 5x WT (8 levels). Measure sigma_z/L by live-cell time-lapse + automated FtsZ-ring localization. Expected: U-shaped curve with minimum at approximately 1x WT MinD. Critical: bifurcation from traveling to standing wave oscillation (measured by spatial Fourier analysis) should coincide with onset of sigma_z/L increase. Temperature-shift prediction: at 25 C (reduced membrane diffusion), C* shifts lower, wild-type MinD may fall above C* — sigma_z/L should increase at 25 C even without MinD perturbation.
hypotheses.experimental_timeline: CRISPRi titration + live imaging: 3-5 months. Temperature-shift test: 1-2 months additional.
hypotheses.groundedness: MEDIUM — MinCDE mode transitions [GROUNDED: Fei & Bhatt 2015, PLOS Comput Biol; Huang et al. 2003, PNAS]; Turing bifurcation in MinDE [GROUNDED: Tostevin & Howard 2006, Biophys J]; sigma_z/L ~3% WT [GROUNDED: imaging studies]; Pareto frontier quantification [PARAMETRIC: derived from mode analysis]
hypotheses.why_stronger: Resolves the Fei & Bhatt contradiction instead of ignoring it. Converts the Critic's most important objection (non-monotonic dissipation-precision) into the central prediction. Introduces Pareto-frontier TUR as theoretical advance. Revises efficiency estimate from vague 15-25x to ~3-5x above pattern-instability-limited floor. Bifurcation-coincidence test is novel and immediately testable with CRISPRi + fluorescence imaging.
